question: Joseph Lister (1827-1912) demonstrated that postoperative mortality dropped from 16 fatalities in 35 procedures to 6 fatalities in 40 procedures after adopting antiseptic surgical techniques. Determine the risks of postoperative mortality in each group. Then, test the proportions for inequality.
answer:
$$
 egin{aligned}
& \hat{p}_1=0.1500 \
& \hat{p}_2=0.457143
\end{aligned}
$$


Hypothesis test
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: p_1=p_2$ versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: p_1 
eq p_2$
B. Test statistic. $z_{	ext {stat }}=2.91$
C. $P$-value. $P=0.0036$, providing highly significant evidence against the null hypothesis.
D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against $H_0$ is significant at $\alpha=0.01$ (reject $H_0$ ).
